pattern: A monotonic-stack greedy that keeps the result lexicographically smallest by popping larger characters when they still appear later, including each distinct letter exactly once.
hints: Every distinct character must appear once, so the only freedom is the order, and you want it as small as possible.
A character already placed can be removed in favor of a smaller one only if it will appear again later in the string.
Maintain a stack and a count of remaining occurrences; before pushing a new character, pop any stack-top that is larger and still occurs later, and skip characters already on the stack.

edge_inputs.description: second example with a repeated later character
all identical characters collapse to one
already sorted distinct characters stay as is
a smaller letter appears only early so it cannot be deferred

stress.description: random strings over a small alphabet to force many pop decisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alpha = 'abcdef'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
